blocks.body: No, none, neither, either are quantifiers expressing absence or choice between two options. No and none mean "none", and neither and either are used when talking about two objects.

{ "title": "10. Brief synopsis", "items": [ "No is the signifier "none" before a noun: no money, no students.", "None is the pronoun "none", used alone or with of: none came, none of them.", "Neither — "neither option, neither of them" (negative): neither option, neither of them.", "Either — "any of the two" (positive): either option, either of them.", "Neither... nor... — "no... no..." (double negation).", "Either... or... — "or... or..." (choice of two).", "Neither/Either — only for two options. For more - use none.", "Neither already contains a negation - do not add not!" ] }
